title: Energy affordability
description: Real oil-price momentum and energy costs relative to household income and GDP show whether energy is becoming harder to afford.
plainLanguageSummary: Real oil-price momentum and energy costs relative to household income and GDP show whether energy is becoming harder to afford.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Burden shares divide energy expenditure by household disposable income or nominal GDP; price and income series use year-over-year change.
calculation.explanation: Aggregate burden can hide household distribution, regional prices, substitution, and policy support.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: Aggregate burden can hide household distribution, regional prices, substitution, and policy support.

methodology.formula: Burden shares divide energy expenditure by household disposable income or nominal GDP; price and income series use year-over-year change.
methodology.notes: Aggregate burden can hide household distribution, regional prices, substitution, and policy support.
